title: Merremia cissoides
intro: Merremia cissoides, also known as the woodrose, is a tropical vine native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Merremia cissoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Merremia cissoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Merremia cissoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Merremia, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family Convolvulaceae is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as morning glories and bindweeds.
